messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: imho we should not create/promote push-BOTs; was Meshtastic not full of telemetry most of us did not care about and one of many reasons to start with and build Meshcore? And does it still work perfectly? If users starting to use push-BOTs excessively and disrupt the Mesh I know other people will want to kill these kind of messages at repeater level. "It's one o'clock and time for lunch Bum-dee-dum, dee-dum" (CET). (edited)
messages.content_html: imho we should not create/promote push-BOTs; was Meshtastic not full of telemetry most of us did not care about and one of many reasons to start with and build Meshcore? And does it still work perfectly? If users starting to use push-BOTs excessively and disrupt the Mesh I know other people will want to kill these kind of messages at repeater level. messages.author: abbie
messages.text: Could always ask the bot personally
messages.content_html: Could always ask the bot personally

messages.author: Wouter-Jan
messages.text: If you need to ask the BOT it is a pull-BOT (action->reaction), push-BOT equals unrequested. And to be honest my Python skills are so bad to determine which one this specific example is. So please do not see my remark as a personal attack on the creator (I would not dare) but a observation that push-BOTs can become as worse or worser than then the old abundancy of telemetry in Meshtastic.

abbie
Could always ask the bot personally

messages.author: abbie
messages.text: i was trying to create a framework for good manners in that aspect, and ended up accidentally creating the FCC In trying to do this in the small town I was in, there would have been absolutely no issue because low traffic and the people wanted an announced weather in the mornings.
